title: Mental Health and Substance Abuse Social Workers
description: Assess and treat individuals with mental, emotional, or substance abuse problems, including abuse of alcohol, tobacco, and/or other drugs. Activities may include individual and group therapy, crisis intervention, case management, client advocacy, prevention, and education.

tasks.text: Counsel clients in individual or group sessions to assist them in dealing with substance abuse, mental or physical illness, poverty, unemployment, or physical abuse.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with counselors, physicians, or nurses to plan or coordinate treatment, drawing on social work experience and patient needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor, evaluate, and record client progress with respect to treatment goals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Interview clients, review records, conduct assessments, or confer with other professionals to evaluate the mental or physical condition of clients or patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise or direct other workers who provide services to clients or patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Modify treatment plans according to changes in client status.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist clients in adhering to treatment plans, such as setting up appointments, arranging for transportation to appointments, or providing support.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate clients or community members about mental or physical illness, abuse, medication, or available community resources.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Counsel or aid family members to assist them in understanding, dealing with, or supporting the client or patient.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Increase social work knowledge by reviewing current literature, conducting social research, or attending seminars, training workshops, or classes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Refer patient, client, or family to community resources for housing or treatment to assist in recovery from mental or physical illness, following through to ensure service efficacy.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan or conduct programs to prevent substance abuse, combat social problems, or improve health or counseling services in community.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or advise on social policy or assist in community development.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
